NY.condition: exempt if item price < 110.00; taxable if item price >= 110.00
NY.notes: Threshold is evaluated against the price the customer actually pays (after discounts). In certain NY counties, clothing under $110 is exempt from county tax but may still be subject to city tax — however for this project, treat the threshold as applying uniformly across all jurisdictions.

tax_holiday.notes: Holiday threshold based on discounted selling price

groceries.description: Unprepared food items for home consumption

prepared_food.description: Ready-to-eat meals, hot food, restaurant food

CA.notes: Candy is considered food in CA
Digital goods generally not taxable in CA

medical_supplies.description: Over-the-counter medicines, medical devices, first aid

HI.notes: HI taxes most items including medical supplies

school_supplies.description: Notebooks, pens, pencils, binders, backpacks used for school

shipping.description: Shipping and handling charges. This is a special category — items with this category represent the shipping line on the order. They follow the shipping_rules section, not normal taxability rules.

ewaste_fee.notes: eWaste fees are never subject to sales tax and never receive discounts. They CAN be refunded.

tire_fee.notes: Tire fees are never subject to sales tax and never receive discounts. They CAN be refunded.

retail_delivery_fee.description: State-mandated retail delivery fee applied to shipped orders
retail_delivery_fee.notes: Retail delivery fees are never subject to sales tax, never receive discounts, and are NEVER refundable — even in full returns.

core_charge.description: Refundable deposit for returning used parts (auto parts, batteries). Charged at purchase, refunded when old part returned.
core_charge.notes: Core charges ARE subject to sales tax (unlike other fees) but NEVER receive discounts. They CAN be refunded.

gratuity.notes: Gratuities are not subject to sales tax and never receive discounts. They CAN be refunded.

fee_handling.description: Fee-type items (is_fee=true) have special rules that differ from regular product categories
fee_handling.discount_exclusion: Items with excluded_from_discounts=true must NEVER receive any portion of line-item or order-level discounts. Order-level discount allocation should skip these items entirely — they are excluded from both the allocation numerator and denominator.
fee_handling.excise_items: Items with is_excise=true are government-mandated fees. They follow their own taxability rules (some are taxable like core_charge, most are not) but are excluded from discount calculations.
fee_handling.refund_rules: Only items with non_refundable=true cannot be returned. All other fee items can be returned and refunded normally.

NY.notes: Shipping and delivery charges are taxable

CA.condition: Taxable only if the shipped items are taxable. If all items are exempt, shipping is exempt. If mixed, shipping tax is proportional to taxable items.
CA.notes: Proportional calculation uses post-discount item values. Fee-type items are excluded from the proportional calculation (only regular product items count).

TX.notes: Shipping and delivery charges are taxable

PA.notes: Shipping and delivery charges are taxable

HI.notes: Shipping is ALWAYS taxable in Hawaii, even when shipped items are exempt

line_discounts.description: Per-item discounts reduce the taxable amount of that specific item
line_discounts.application: Subtract discount from (price * quantity) to get taxable amount. Discount cannot reduce taxable amount below zero.
line_discounts.excluded_items: Line discounts CANNOT be applied to items with excluded_from_discounts=true. If a request includes a non-zero discount on such an item, return an error.
line_discounts.notes: Discounts are applied BEFORE threshold evaluation (NY clothing, TX holiday)

order_discounts.description: Order-level discounts are allocated proportionally across eligible items only
order_discounts.eligible_items: Only items that are NOT excluded_from_discounts AND are NOT categorically exempt (e.g., groceries in a state where groceries are always exempt). Fee-type items and shipping are always excluded from allocation.
order_discounts.notes: Allocate order discount to each eligible item as: item_share = order_discount * (item_line_value / total_eligible_value). Round allocated amounts to nearest cent per item. If rounding causes total allocated to differ from order_discount, adjust the largest eligible item's allocation.

return_rules.description: Rules for calculating tax refunds on returned items
return_rules.rate_source_notes: Returns always use the tax rates from the ORIGINAL transaction, not current rates.

partial_returns.description: When only some items are returned, only those items' tax is refunded
partial_returns.shipping_refund_notes: Shipping tax refund is proportional to value returned. Calculate proportion using the original order's item values (excluding fee-type items from both numerator and denominator).

return_rules.non_refundable_notes: Items in non-refundable categories cannot be returned. The API should reject return requests that include non-refundable items.
return_rules.fee_refunds: Fee-type items that are NOT non_refundable (ewaste_fee, tire_fee, core_charge, gratuity) CAN be returned. Their tax (if any) is refunded. They are excluded from shipping refund proportion calculation.

marketplace_facilitator.description: When an order is placed through a marketplace (e.g., Amazon, eBay), the marketplace is responsible for collecting tax, not the seller.
marketplace_facilitator.effect: When is_marketplace_order=true, the marketplace has already collected and remitted tax. The API should return zero tax for the entire order regardless of nexus, exemptions, or any other rules. This overrides everything.
marketplace_facilitator.notes: This is a simple flag check that should short-circuit all other logic. If the marketplace already collected tax, there is nothing for the seller to collect.

exemption_certificates.description: Valid exemption certificates remove tax liability

resale.description: Buyer is purchasing for resale, not end use
resale.effect: All sales tax removed (state + local) on all items. Fee-type items follow their own rules (excise items are not affected by resale certificates — if they are taxable, they remain taxable).

nonprofit.description: Buyer is a qualifying nonprofit organization
nonprofit.effect: State tax removed on regular items; local taxes may still apply depending on state. Fee-type excise items are not affected.

rounding_rules.description: Tax should be calculated and rounded per line item (quantity * price * rate), then summed. Round to nearest cent using standard rounding (0.005 rounds up).
